review:
Price keeps climbing. Running ads for ICE is screwed up. Worst of all they only pay music artists between $0.003 cents and $0.005 cents per song played all the way through.
It’s ok to have on the go, but for the same price of $15 (and consistently climbing) you could actually buy physical media, to keep, forever. I cannot, in good conscience, advise investing in a company or service that does this. If you don’t keep paying up, any music you downloaded to your phone for offline use is gone. When you do the math for what we pay vs what they use our money for, it’s really, really messed up… it’s just wrong.
If you like listening to podcasts, that’s different. This is perfect for that, and the podcasters make way more money. Personally, I do not. I just like to know how my apps work when it comes to copyrighted material.
title: Like music? Buy it and own it for the same $

review:
Has a good variety of music. Issues are that they aren’t secure— recent trending songs such as Terranova get hacked. 

You can’t select what you want to listen to, you can’t skip ads (you get 2 each 1-2 songs), and want all your money.

Developers failed to pay artists so they started giving us unstoppable ads, limited selection of our music, forced shuffles, etc. Find better ways to pay artists and earn money. I couldn’t believe picking a rock song would bring me to K-POP that isn’t made by PSY. 

Not only they are using our money to poorly pay other artists, they are funding useless projects/updates having to do with the app, such as virtual DJs and AI. 

Wow… get better. I don’t recommend the app. Listen to music on the radio or save it as a MP3.

Given enough time - all software decays. Spotify is a prime example of this phenomenon  especially in the age of AI. When you're building what's basically a utility/tool for a lot of people at this point - stability must be a foundational pillar in my opinion. But the quality bar has dropped drastically in the last two quarters of 2026. 

Home screen ui issues, random app crashes, terrible apple tv app experience. It's all over the place. Please add relevant bad customer experience metrics like app abandoned, session abandon, app force close. I bet these are up significantly. And please consider taking some basic goals to bring them down over next few quarters. Shipping features for the sake of shipping features ends badly - I speak from experience as a veteran iOS dev. If you wanna ship more - do it in a new standalone app, why ruin the main app?
title: Buggy mess - quality and stability of the app feel off a cliff in 2026

review:
I understand having a premium version and a free version, but I don’t understand making the free version completely unuseable for being abroad??? 

In my case, I ALWAYS use a VPN as to ensure my E-safety, but with this application, I now can’t listen to music with a VPN? I haven’t a clue how to check “what country my profile is in” and I don’t want to sacrifice such a big part of my internet safety to listen to music.

This hasn’t always been a problem; infact, I’ve never had this problem before. Spotify would just give me advertisements in a different language. That’s fine, I don’t mind the advertisements. Infact, I was on spotify just a few days ago and it played as normal. Today, I open the app and it tells me I’m listening internationally which is a premium benefit?? What??

Also, you can’t even choose what song you want to listen to in the moment. That’s the whole point of a music app, is it not? To listen to the music you want; why is this a benefit? 

I don’t know; I love music. Ever since I first realised it was a whole thing on the radios as a child. This VPN thing has really annoyed me though.
title: Too restrictive without premium

I dislike the lack of functionality in the macbook app, compared to the iphone app.  Also why on earth is there not an "add to play next" type button/option, as opposed to merely a "add to queue" option? It is so annoying that when you already have a long queue, you can only add an item to the end of the queue, and the you have to manually move that item all the way up to the beginning of the long queue just because you actually want hear that item *next*.  Furthermore, I want to be able to create *multiple* queues. That is because everyday I spend time and effort creating a long queue of podcasts I want to listen to for that day, BUT I don't want to listen to then all in one sitting -- often I want to listen to various music throughout the day too. So please let me create a podcast queue and *seperate* music queue (and, for the avoidance of doubt, that is NOT the same as creating multiple playlists because I don't want to keep these queues as playlists once the day is finished).

review: I’ve rated Spotify before and previously gave it 5 stars. However, there is one thing I would like to mention that lowers its rating. The algorithm that recommends music to you is not great. I don’t know if it’s a cost issue or just that the algorithm isn’t great but it will recommend me really random songs from genres that I have never listened to in my life. I’m not against broadening my music taste however it becomes frustrating when you want to listen to a specific music genre and all it recommends are genres you have no care for. All that being said it is a genuinely good app and despite popular opinion, I personally love their Spotify wrapped. I recommend people to download this app because it genuinely is worth it. If you do not have Spotify premium the adds can be bothersome however there are other options like a family plan that are cheaper than Spotify premium that get rid of the ads. Overall it’s a great app and a worthwhile investment!!

review:
Spotify used to be great, but now I’m finding the app incredibly confusing. There are so many tabs, and some of them seem to appear more than once. I also have to scroll endlessly through album icons that I never selected. I’m talking pages and pages of them.

The user experience is a mess. And what the hell is the new icon anyway?

When I search for something, I get endless results that have nothing to do with what I’m looking for. Classical music is especially a nightmare. If a piece has more than one movement, I have to click on every single track just to hear it or see the full title before I can choose the one I want.

The only reason I’m still using Spotify is because I’ve built playlists over the last 10 years. Ironically, those playlists are so hard to find that I can spend the first three minutes in the app just trying to locate them.

I’d much rather see my own playlist icons than random miscellaneous junk I don’t care about. It would be so nice if the app were simpler, cleaner, and actually useful.

Spotify, we can do better.
title: Spotify: we need to talk

review:
While I’m a long-term fan and heavy user of the platform, persistent dissatisfaction has accompanied the experience on 3 main points.   Firstly the interface continues in a dated idea of how modern UI can be.  Second, the home page and the suggestions of what I might like have always offered me so much of what I have never and won’t ever listen to.  Lastly that singular option of a ridiculous voice and manner of the ai DJ.  It’s absurd and jarring.  
That said, other aspects of the algorithm are great.  Discover Weekly has given me so much over the years, so for now it remains the home of my countless playlists.

review:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

J’utilise Spotify depuis longtemps et c’est de loin la meilleure application de musique que j’ai essayée. L’interface est simple, moderne et très facile à utiliser. J’adore pouvoir découvrir de nouvelles chansons grâce aux playlists personnalisées et aux recommandations qui correspondent vraiment à mes goûts musicaux.

La qualité du son est excellente et il y a un immense choix de musique, de balados et de contenus pour tous les styles. J’aime aussi le fait de pouvoir créer mes propres playlists et les partager avec mes amis. L’application fonctionne très bien et je l’utilise tous les jours, que ce soit à la maison, à l’école, en voiture ou pendant mes activités.

Spotify rend l’écoute de musique agréable et pratique. Les mises à jour apportent régulièrement de nouvelles fonctionnalités intéressantes et l’expérience s’améliore constamment. Je recommande cette application à tous ceux qui aiment la musique. Merci à toute l’équipe Spotify pour cette excellente application !

review: I’ve paid for Spotify premium for like seven years and it was fine, until I discovered that there was no way for me to filter out and avoid all of the terrible AI music that is clogging up the platform. So I cancelled my premium for this reason, and I’m infuriated to learn that you can only play albums in the intended order on your mobile app if you have premium which I think is a really ridiculous feature. I accept that if I don’t have premium I have to listen to ads, but to not be able to play an album in order is infuriating. I will be finding another music service. Which is a shame because I liked Spotify until I couldn’t avoid AI music and couldn’t play my albums in order. Ai and predatory subscription models ruin everything.

review:
I’ve never had any complaints about Spotify, until the last few weeks. 

It is now regularly stopping playing music. It says, “We’re having trouble playing this song. Try going online…”

It happens even when I’m online.

If I kill the app and restart it, the same song that was failing will pick up exactly where it left off. I can also start playing a new playlist.

Having this happen when I’m driving means I can’t play music until I can safely pull over and restart the app. It doesn’t matter if I’m using CarPlay or not, it happens either way. 

It happens to everybody in my family, as well. There’s no explanation that makes sense, except for a new bug being introduced that’s impacting playback.

review:
Dear Spotify Team,

I would like to provide feedback regarding a recent change to Spotify’s CarPlay experience.

Previously, my playlists in CarPlay appeared in alphabetical order, making it easy and safe to locate the playlist I wanted while driving. Recently, the playlist order appears to have changed, and playlists are no longer displayed alphabetically.

This has made navigation significantly more difficult, especially when managing a large number of playlists. The current ordering requires more scrolling and attention, which can be distracting while driving.

I would appreciate it if Spotify could either:

1. Restore alphabetical playlist sorting in CarPlay, or
2. Provide users with the option to choose their preferred playlist sorting method (Alphabetical, Custom, Recently Played, etc.).

I believe this would greatly improve usability and safety for drivers who rely on CarPlay.

Thank you for considering this feedback. I hope Spotify will address this issue in a future update.
